name: Moroccan Turkey Tagine Stew
intro: A classic Moroccan tagine stew with warm spices, vegetables, and dried fruit adds leftover turkey for a twist on a traditional dish.

ingredients.item: (15 ounces) of can garbanzo beans, drained, rinsed
(14.5 ounces) of can Hunt's Diced Tomatoes, undrained
chopped pitted prunes or whole raisins
Greek yogurt and hot cooked couscous, optional

method: Heat oil in a Dutch oven or large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onion; cook 3 minutes or until tender, stirring occasionally. Add carrots and parsnips; cook 5 minutes or until crisp-tender. Stir in garlic and ras el hanout seasoning; cook 1 minute or until fragrant .
Stir in beans, undrained tomatoes, broth, water, apricots, and prunes. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally.
Stir in turkey; heat 5 minutes more or until hot. Top each serving with cilantro. If desired, top with yogurt and serve with couscous on the side.
